issue:
Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name. 
Can you let me know how to do this?
subject: How to Remove a User
response:
To remove a team member from HackerRank, please note that you must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to perform this action. Here are the steps to manage team members:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account using your credentials.
2. Select your profile/account settings.
3. Navigate to the Team Members section under Settings > Teams Management.

Please note that the 'remove' option should be available from the team members management area. If you are not seeing the option when selecting the three dots next to their name, it is possible that your account may not have the required Company Admin or Team Admin permissions to perform this action. Please verify your role and permissions. If you believe your permissions are correct and the issue persists, a support agent may need to investigate further.

For full details, you can refer to the HackerRank support article on managing team members: https://support.hackerrank.com/articles/2203617737-manage-team-members
justification: The corpus excerpt for 'Manage Team Members' directly addresses this use case — it covers adding/removing team members and updating roles, and notes that Company Admin or Team Admin access is required. The user's issue (not seeing the remove option) is likely a permissions issue, which is addressed by the prerequisite noted in the article. No escalation is needed as this is a standard product usage question covered by the corpus.

issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
subject: Subscription pause
response:
Thank you for reaching out! The **Pause Subscription** feature is available on HackerRank for individual self-serve plan subscribers. It allows you to temporarily pause your subscription instead of canceling it, giving you the flexibility to resume whenever you're ready to hire again.

To use this feature, please ensure the prerequisites are met (including having an active subscription). You can find full details and steps in the Pause Subscription support article here: https://support.hackerrank.com/articles/5157311476-pause-subscription

If you are on an enterprise or team plan (rather than a self-serve individual plan), the pause feature may not be available to you. In that case, you may want to consider canceling your subscription — cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing cycle, and you retain access to your plan features until then. You can cancel by logging into HackerRank for Work, going to **Manage Subscription** from the profile dropdown, and clicking **Cancel Plan**.

Please let us know if you need any further assistance!
justification: The corpus contains a dedicated 'Pause Subscription' article (5157311476) that directly addresses this request. The feature exists for self-serve plan subscribers. Since this is a straightforward product guidance question and no billing dispute, fraud, or sensitive situation is involved, a 'replied' status is appropriate. The cancel subscription articles were also referenced as a fallback option in case the user is not on a self-serve plan.

issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response:
Good news! You can update the name on your HackerRank certificate. Please note the following important points before proceeding:

- You can only update your name **once per account**.
- When you update your name, the change applies to **all** your certificates.
- After updating, you **cannot change it again**.

To update your certificate name, please visit the HackerRank Certifications FAQs page at https://help.hackerrank.com/articles/8941367927-certifications-faqs for the step-by-step instructions (including a visual guide). Make sure you are logged into your HackerRank Community account before making the change.
justification: The corpus excerpt from the Certifications FAQs article directly addresses the user's request to update their certificate name. It confirms this is possible (once per account) and references step-by-step instructions. This is a standard product issue that can be resolved with self-service guidance from the corpus, so no escalation is needed.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
Thank you for reaching out! Anthropic does crawl publicly available web data as part of its model development and other functions. However, as a website owner, you have the ability to block Anthropic's crawler. According to our support documentation, Anthropic uses different robots to enable website owner transparency and choice, and there are three robots that Anthropic uses with corresponding opt-out mechanisms.

For full details on how to block each specific crawler, please visit the dedicated support article here: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler

Additionally, you can learn more about Anthropic's broader privacy practices at our Privacy Center: https://privacy.anthropic.com/

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response:
Thank you for reaching out! As a professor looking to set up the Claude LTI key for your students, there is a dedicated guide available for this. According to our support documentation, the article 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure' covers exactly this use case. The steps are intended for Claude for Education administrators and Learning Management Systems (LMS) administrators.

Here are the initial steps from the guide for creating a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas:

1. In Canvas, sign in as an **administrator** and navigate to **Admin -> Developer Keys**.
2. Click **'+ Developer Key'** then **'+ LTI Key'**.
3. Enter the following:
   - **Key Name:** Claude LTI
   - **Description:** (as specified in the full article)

Please note that you will need **administrator access** in Canvas to complete this setup. If you are not a Canvas administrator at your institution, you may need to coordinate with your institution's LMS or IT administrator.

For the full setup instructions, you can refer to the article at: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure

If you need further assistance, feel free to reach out!
